evals.prompt: Two branches both add the same task_cell key to a motor registry JSON but with different owning motors (duplicate-authority claim) -- produces a real git merge conflict.
evals.expected_output: Agent identifies this as an L1 one-writer-per-task-cell violation, does not silently pick a side, preserves all non-conflicting entries, and escalates rather than declaring it merge-ready.
expectations: Identifies that the conflict is two different motors/owners claiming the same task_cell key, not just a generic JSON text conflict
Does NOT silently pick one side's value for the conflicting key and call it resolved
Explicitly names this as a duplicate-ownership / one-writer-per-task-cell governance problem needing a human/registry decision, not a normal merge
Preserves all non-conflicting registry entries from both branches with no data loss
Does not claim the PR is merge-ready without an explicit escalation or flagged open question about which owner is correct

evals.prompt: Two branches each add a receipt file at the exact same path/filename but with different proof content -- a real git merge conflict on a file added by both sides.
evals.expected_output: Agent preserves both receipts (proof-of-done claims), never drops one, flags the identical-filename collision as suspicious, and disambiguates rather than picking a winner.
expectations: Recognizes both receipts represent real proof-of-done claims that must both be preserved
Does not delete or silently overwrite one receipt in favor of the other
Flags the identical-filename collision itself as suspicious (possible duplicate-motor claim) rather than treating it as an ordinary text conflict
Resolves by disambiguating (e.g. renaming/keeping both under distinct paths) rather than picking a single winner
Does not declare the conflict fully resolved without noting the collision is worth investigating further

evals.prompt: A merge conflict inside a doc whose header explicitly says Status: LOCKED with a named Authority -- two branches propose different values for a locked policy line.
evals.expected_output: Agent notices the LOCKED marker, refuses to unilaterally resolve the locked content, and escalates to a human/founder for sign-off.
expectations: Explicitly notices/mentions the LOCKED status marker in the document header
Declines to unilaterally pick a merged value for the locked content itself
Recommends escalating to a human/founder for sign-off before any resolution is finalized
Does not present a self-resolved 'final' merged version of the locked line as done/ready-to-merge

evals.prompt: A merge conflict in a machine-generated sidecar JSON file (produced by a scanner script) where two branches produced different scan output for the same source doc.
evals.expected_output: Agent recognizes the file is generated output and recommends regenerating via the source script instead of hand-merging the JSON.
expectations: Recognizes the conflicted file is machine-generated output, not hand-authored content
Recommends regenerating the file by re-running the generator/scanner script rather than hand-merging the JSON
If it does provide a manual merge as a stopgap, explicitly flags it as temporary and to be replaced by regeneration
Does not silently hand-craft merged JSON and present it as equivalent to what the generator would produce
